name: Puebla (Mexico)

colors.symbolism: the field and details of the arms

description: A white field with the coat of arms of the state of Puebla centred.
history: Puebla has no officially adopted state flag; the design shown — a white field bearing the state coat of arms — is in de facto use to represent the state.

features.symbolism: A white field charged with the state coat of arms, in the typical pattern of Mexican state flags.

The arms commemorate the Battle of Puebla of 5 May 1862, when Mexican forces under General Ignacio Zaragoza defeated the invading French army, together with references to the volcanoes Popocatepetl and Iztaccihuatl that overlook the state, and to the colonial city's title "Heroica".
